title: EU Circumvention Investigations/Cold-Rolled Stainless Steel Flat Products
bodyHtml:
the European Commission’s Directorate-General for Trade (the Commission) has been conducting investigations related to the possible circumvention of the anti-dumping and countervailing measures applied to imports of cold-rolled stainless steel flat products originating from Indonesia (under HS codes 7219.31.00, 7219.32.10, 7219.32.90, 7219.33.10, 7219.33.90, 7219.34.10, 7219.34.90, 7219.35.10, 7219.35.90, 7219.90.20, 7219.90.80, 7220.20.21, 7220.20.29, 7220.20.41, 7220.20.49, 7220.20.81, 7220.20.89, 7220.90.20, 7220.90.80) by exporting these products via Taiwan, Vietnam, and Türkiye.

According to a letter received from the Ministry of Trade, the final decisions of these investigations were published in the EU Official Journal on May 7, 2024. The letter states that, upon review of the final decision of the anti-dumping investigation, it was concluded that Türkiye did not circumvent the anti-dumping measure. However, upon review of the final decision of the countervailing measure investigation, it was found that one firm circumvented the countervailing measure, while another firm that requested exemption was found not to have circumvented the measure.

In this context, it was decided to extend the countervailing duty measure to cold-rolled stainless steel flat products shipped from Turkey (whether declared as originating from Türkiye or not), with  the exception of those produced by the companies listed in Article 1 of the Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/1268. The countervailing duty rate to be applied to these products by the EU was set at 20.5%. For the exemption to be applied, the importer must provide:

-A commercial invoice including a declaration by the exporter-producer and a manufacturer's declaration for direct export sales, as listed in Annex 1 of the final decision, if the product is purchased directly from the exporter-producer.

-A commercial invoice including a declaration by the producer for sales through a trader or other intermediary legal entity, and a commercial invoice issued by the trader or other intermediary legal entity to the importer for indirect export sales, as listed in Annex 2 of the final decision, if the product is purchased from a trader regardless of whether the trader is based in Türkiye.

The final decisions of the investigations can be accessed through the following links:

Click here for the final decision of the anti-dumping investigation.

Click here for the final decision of the countervailing measure investigation.
